description: Mexican drug cartel based in Ciudad Juárez, Chihuahua, founded around the 1970s by Pablo Acosta Villarreal, Rafael Aguilar Guajardo, Amado Carrillo Fuentes, and Vicente Carrillo Fuentes. It controls drug trafficking routes into the United States and is known for extreme violence, including decapitations. Its armed wing is La Línea. The cartel declined after the death of Amado Carrillo Fuentes in 1997 but remains active.

country_links.evidence_quote: controlling a large percentage of the cocaine traffic from Mexico into the United States... the Barrio Azteca street gang operates in Mexico and in Texan cities such as El Paso, Dallas, and Houston, as well as in New Mexico and Arizona.
country_links.context: The Juárez Cartel controls cocaine trafficking from Mexico into the United States and operates through the Barrio Azteca gang in Texan cities such as El Paso, Dallas, and Houston, as well as in New Mexico and Arizona.
